observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

exert all effort to find and furnish sufficient evidence and proper documentation for all outstanding Payables pursuant to Sections 4(6) and 111(1) of PD No. 1445;




notify in writing all payees of outstanding payables with complete documentation beyond two years of their claims from the Municipality; and




revert all balances of Payable accounts outstanding for more than two years to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant started to find documentation for all outstanding payables.




Identified payees of outstanding payables with complete documentation were notified of their claims. 




The Municipal Accountant has started the analysis of these payables including the gathering of the supporting documents.
observations.reason:
There were no available records / documents to support some payables which have been outstanding for more than 10 years already.

Payees who have proper documentation has claimed their payments. However, some payables still do not have available records / documents.

Schedule of payables still showed long-oustanding balances from CY 2014 and prior years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

exert all effort to find and furnish sufficient evidence and proper documentation for all outstanding Receivables in order to enforce collection and to preserve all related documents for proper turnover in accordance with Section 111 (1) of PD No. 1445;




maintain subsidiary ledgers and proper aging for each payor/agency to monitor the need for estimation on the allowance for impairment of receivables that remained dormant/uncollectible for many years in accordance with IPSAS 29; and

request for the approval of this Commission for the write-off/derecognition of the dormant balances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 as amended by COA Circular No. 2023-008.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant started to gather documentation for outstanding receivables in order to request approval of the Commission for the write-off/ derecognition of the dormant balances.


The Municipal Accountant started to prepare subsidiary ledgers and proper aging for each payor/agency.


No action taken.
observations.reason:
There were no available records / documents to support some receivables which have been outstanding for more than 10 years already.

No estimation on allowance for impairment in the FS.

No request for write-off was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the contractor to submit immediately the OR, AR, and an approved contract to support the validity and propriety of transactions made and to enable us to make a decision in audit. Henceforth, ensure the completeness of documentation on any claim against government funds in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management informed the private law firm to submit the required documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the:

Acting Municipal Accountant to require the accountable officers to liquidate immediately the existing cash advances and submit the liquidation report to COA and cease from approving additional cash advances to employees who have prior unliquidated cash advances,



Municipal Treasurer to apply for the increase of fidelity bonds of the concerned accountable officers.
observations.managementAction:
Management informed the accountable officers to promptly liquidate cash advances.





Fidelity bonds were updated and applied for the concerned accountable officers.
observations.reason: One of the officers was not able to liquidate her cash advance due to loss of receipts during her seminar.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to oversee the allowed timeframe for advertisement /posting of all the succeeding procurements in the PhilGEPs in accordance with Section 54.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to render a more competitive bidding.
observations.managementAction: The BAC reviewed the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and observed the allowed timeframe for posting in some procurements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

stop from incurring expenses through reimbursements; and



establish a petty cash fund to defray expenses involving minimal amounts.
observations.managementAction:
Only expenses which were emergency in nature were paid through reimbursements. 

No action taken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the:
Municipal Treasurer to prepare a Schedule of Unreleased Checks particularly at the end of the year and provide a copy to the Municipal Accountant; and
Municipal Accountant to restore the cash equivalent of all unreleased checks by preparing a JEV at the end of the year based on the submitted schedule from the Municipal Treasurer and thereafter, in the ensuing year, another JEV shall be made to reverse the previous entries made and recognize the availability of the checks for release. This is to ensure the fair presentation of the account balances in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Management direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant-Designate to obligate and promptly release the TLB of already separated personnel amounting to ₱994,255.80, provided that the complete documentary requirements as listed in the COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 are submitted, and to revert the remaining ₱1,834,135.35 unexpended PS classified under continuing appropriations into savings which could be subjected for reappropriation in order to strictly follow the validity of appropriations in accordance with the DBM NBC for the FY.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to:
draw adjusting entries concerning the mentioned accounts while the adjustments in income and expenses are to be closed in “Prior Period Adjustments”, in order to fairly and completely present the financial statements in the CY 2024, and
strengthen controls in recording JEVs and ledgers by being meticulous in reviewing the supporting documents and by ensuring the account title which meets the definition and recognition criteria for the transactions that are being recorded – safeguarding the government accounts against inaccurate or misleading information.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to meticulously review the accounting documents and records in order to accurately report balances in the financial statements in accordance with the definitions and recognition criteria for assets, liabilities, revenue and expenses set out in IPSAS 1 and to draw the necessary adjusting entries for the elimination of abnormal balances.

We recommended that the Management direct the BAC to strictly adhere with Sections 54.2 and 54.3 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on the posting of Invitation or Request for Submission of Price Quotations/Proposals, Notice of Award, Contract or Purchase Order, including Notice to Proceed for alternative methods of procurement with ABC above ₱50,000.00. This is to promote transparency and ensure accountability in the procurement process.
Further, Management should be guided of the New Government Procurement Act prescribed by RA No. 12009 and its IRR. The Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) and all heads of procuring entities (HOPE), procuring entities and the BACs shall be given a 3-year transitory period from the approval of the standard forms for procurement to fully comply with the requirements of the New Government Procurement Act.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the BAC and other concerned employees to be meticulous and judicious in the preparation of their Annual Procurement Plan so that procurements made by the Municipality are properly planned, consolidated and executed, and procurements made through alternative methods do not result in splitting of government contracts.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries in the GF books as follows:

Dr. Prior Year Adjustment 545,752.56
Cr. Due to Other Funds 545,752.56
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant effected the required adjusting journal entries.

observations.managementAction: The LCE and other concerned Officials had updated the Local Revenue Code.
The Management disseminated information on the proper procurement procedures and discouraged the payment of goods on reimbursement basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the GSO to submit to the Audit Team: a) Evaluation Report of the Municipal Engineer on the status/physical condition of all the demolished Buildings and Other Structures, indicating therein justifications/reasons for demolition; b) photographs taken before, during and after demolition; c) detailed inventory of salvaged materials of the demolished Buildings and Other Structures; and to strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989 and Section 379 of RA No. 7160 for all future disposal or divestment of properties of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to oversee the allowed timeframe for advertisement/posting of all the succeeding procurements in the PhilGEPs in accordance with Section 54.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to render a more competitive bidding.

We further recommended that the BAC observe transparency in the procurement process through posting the results of public bidding in pursuance to Section 54.3 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

observations.reason:
Procurements were still posted for a period of less than three days in the PhilGEPs.

Implemented
Notice of award, contract and purchase orders were already posted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the GSO to submit to the Audit Team: a) Evaluation Report of the Municipal Engineer on the status/physical condition of all the demolished Buildings and Other Structures, indicating therein justifications/reasons for demolition; b) photographs taken before, during and after demolition; c) detailed inventory of salvaged materials of the demolished Buildings and Other Structures; and to strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989 and Section 379 of RA No. 7160 for all future disposal or divestment of properties of the Municipality.

observations.reason:
Demolished markets are still included in the books and depreciated during the current year. 

Also, we required the Municipal Engineer to submit the Waste Material Report for the Demolished Public Market but has not still submitted as of this writing.

Reiterated under Findings No. 1 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
exert all effort to find and furnish sufficient evidence and proper documentation for all outstanding Payables pursuant to Sections 4(6) and 111(1) of PD No. 1445;
notify in writing all payees of outstanding payables with complete documentation beyond two years of their claims from the Municipality; and
revert all balances of Payable accounts outstanding for more than two years to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
exert all effort to find and furnish sufficient evidence and proper documentation for all outstanding Receivables in order to enforce collection and to preserve all related documents for proper turnover in accordance with Section 111 (1) of PD No. 1445;
maintain subsidiary ledgers and proper aging for each payor/agency to monitor the need for estimation on the allowance for impairment of receivables that remained dormant/uncollectible for many years in accordance with IPSAS 29; and
request for the approval of this Commission for the write-off/derecognition of the dormant balances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 as amended by COA Circular No. 2023-008.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries in the GF books as follows:

Prior Years Adjustment ₱ 545,752.56
Due to Other Funds ₱ 545,752.56

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw the following adjusting journal entries pursuant to RA No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 for fair and accurate presentation of the FS

Transfer of remaining unutilized balance of CY 2023

Revert the erroneous recording of unspent balance of CY 2021 LDRRMF:

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Budget Officer, to prepare the NFS in accordance with the pertinent provisions of IPSAS 1 and COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016 and make certain the propriety and completeness of required disclosures for fair and accurate presentation of the financial statements.
We recommend that the LCE and other concerned municipal officials and employees to prioritize the updating of the Municipality’s LRC, pursuant to Section 191 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that management require the contractor to submit immediately the OR, AR, and an approved contract to support the validity and propriety of transactions made and to enable us to make a decision in audit. Henceforth, ensure the completeness of documentation on any claim against government funds in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that Management instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to:

Determine the appropriate useful life of the assets in accordance to the guidelines set by COA Circular 2017-004, and

Compute and record for the depreciation of the assets as of the current year and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the related balances of the accounts reported in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant determined the useful life of the assets, computed for the depreciation, and subsequently recorded the same in the book of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to observe the rules and regulations promulgated under Item 3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 specifically in the granting of cash advances to those unbonded employees and elected officers to prevent any circumstances for the loss of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer updated the list of bonded officers/ employees and determined employees who, by the nature or circumstance of their work, needs to be granted with cash advance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management through the Acting Municipal Accountant to record succeeding employee salary loans deducted from the general payroll to the Other Payables account and reclassify the existing Loans Payable Domestic account balance in the CY 2022 financial statements as Other Payables.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments on accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to require from the SBM the Affidavit that there was no pending criminal investigation or prosecution against him (RA No. 3019) and submit a copy to the COA for verification.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant required from the SBMs the necessary supporting documents for their TLB claims.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management through the BAC require the contractor to submit a copy of the CSHP duly approved by the DOLE which shall form part of the contract.

In case the contractor will not submit or comply with the said document after the infrastructure project was satisfactorily completed, this would cause management to hold the billing payment.
observations.managementAction: The BAC included in the contracts of the bidders the submission of CSHP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

Determine the appropriate useful life for the specific PPE accounts following the range provided under COA Circular 2017-004; and

Compute and record the depreciation for the current year and make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the understatement of accumulated depreciation in order to present fairly the accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Accountant has provided for the depreciation of some PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop the practice of granting cash advances (except for official travel) to municipal officials and employees who are not bonded pursuant to PD No. 1445 and COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2009-002. It is also recommended that elective officials may not be granted special cash advances except for the purpose of official traveling expenses.
observations.managementAction: Municipal elective officials were no longer granted cash advances for special purpose, except for official travels.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to create an Appraisal Committee to appraise the two units donated ambulance and some equipment, furniture and fixtures in order for the Municipal Accountant to have a basis for the cost of the property to be recorded in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Management created an Appraisal Committee to determine the value of the two units ambulance donated by PCSO.
observations.reason: Deemed implemented as only one unit was recorded in the books, however, the Municipal GSO reported and photographed the ambulances as unserviceable properties for disposal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management insure their properties to be indemnified in case of damages caused by fortuitous events.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer/GSO Designate has applied for the insurance of the Transportation Vehicles of the LGU.
observations.reason: Review of records showed that transportation vehicles of the Municipality are already insured with the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal GSO to (a) prepare an IIRPPE to be inspected and be assessed/appraised by the Agency Appraisal and Inspection Team with the presence of the Auditor and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant; and (b) cause for the disposal of unserviceable properties but if found valuable the same be sold for public auction.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 started consolidating the LGUs unserviceable/demolished properties. After consolidation, IIRPPE will be made.
observations.reason: No IIRPPE was submitted to the Office of the Auditor and no actual disposal of unserviceable properties was undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to inform the payees on their claims from the Municipality and revert to the Government Equity those which are not filed with valid claims that have been outstanding for two years or more.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 started to inform the concerned payees and released some of the retention monies.
observations.reason: Guaranty Deposits Payable as of December 31, 2021 has decreased to ₱1,239,458.82. Review of the submitted schedules revealed that the Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable account still included those projects from 2014 and prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert all oustanding balances of Accounts Payable that are not duly supported with proper documentations and which have remained outstanding for more that two years to the unapprpriated surplus pursuant to Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant started to monitor the claims which are not supported with complete documents and will revert the Payable accounts at the end of the year.
observations.reason: Review of the supporting Schedules and Ageing of Accounts Payable revealed that some of the Payable accounts still included pertains to CY 2011 transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the GSO to submit to the Audit Team: a) Evaluation Report of the Municipal Engineer on the status/physical condition of all the demolished Buildings and Other Structures, indicating therein justifications/reasons for demolition; b) photographs taken before, during and after demolition; c) detailed inventory of salvaged materials of the demolished Buildings and Other Structures; and to strictly follow the provisions of COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989 and Section 379 of RA No. 7160 for all future disposal or divestment of properties of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Engineering is yet to prepare the Evaluation Reports. Pictures of the demolished properties were taken and will be submitted to the Auditor.
observations.reason: The required documents were not yet submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit annually APP-CSE to PS-DBM within the prescribed deadline, thus helping PS-DBM in delivering more efficiently the requirements of the Municipality; and submit/attach a PS Certificate of Non-Availability of stocks if goods were purchase from a private dealer. Non submission of the document would compel the auditor to recommend the disallowance pursuant to paragraph 4.9 of DBM Circular 2013-14. Also, strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 53.5 of IRR of RA No. 9184 and continue its practice of filing the agency procurement request to the PS-DBM as a modest contribution to achieve good governance.
observations.managementAction: The Procurement Service was not used in the procurement of common-use supplies for CY 2021. The Municipality did not purchase common use supplies from the PS-DBM due to the present pandemic situation. Rest assured, Management complies with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to create an Appraisal Committee to appraise the two units donated ambulance and some equipment, furniture and fixtures in order for the Municipal Accountant to have a basis for the cost of the property to be recorded in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by management.
observations.reason: Management has not recognized in the books the two units donated ambulance and its accessories.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management utilize funds received to deliver the much-needed services or equipment to its constituents in accordance with the issued guidelines and laws, for which the fund was granted and to avoid disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Motor Vehicle was purchased in good faith, however Management continue to use the vehicle in accordance with the purpose.
observations.reason: The guidelines set by DILG was not followed. ND No. 2019-001 (18) dated 5/18/2019 was issued

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management insure their properties to be indemnified in case of damages caused by fortuitous events.
observations.managementAction: Treasurer/GSO Designate had made the procedures to apply for the insurance of the properties.
observations.reason: Review of records showed that insurable properties of the Municipality were not yet insured with the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE/Mun. Engineer explain in writing the circumstances surrounding the demolition of the building; Management submit the Inventory and Inspection Report, photographs, as well as the Waste Material Report; and Management to strictly follow the guidelines on the divestment or disposal of property and other assets as provided under COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by management.
observations.reason: We required the Municipal Management to submit the Inventory and the Waste Material Report for the Demolished Public Market but has not submitted as of this writing.

observations.recommendation:
1.7. We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to:

a. Determine the appropriate useful life for the specific PPE accounts
following the range provided under COA Circular 2017-004; and
b. Compute and record the depreciation for the current year and make the
necessary adjusting entries to correct the understatement of
accumulated depreciation in order to present fairly the accounts.

2.10. We recommended that Management require the Acting Municipal
Accountant:

a. To prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries in the GF books by
debiting Prior Year Adjustment and Crediting Due to Other Fund in the
amount of ₱228,677.56; and
b. Undertake the transfer of ₱933.92 from SEF to GF and prepare the
adjusting journal entry in the SEF books by debiting Due to Other Funds
and crediting Cash in Bank.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and provide the Municipal Accountant a copy of the certified list of taxpayers and the corresponding amount due and collectible for the year as the basis of the latter in recording/book up the RPT and SET Receivables Accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer already provided the Certified List of Taxpayers to the Municipal Accountant. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant recorded the RPT and SET receivables.
observations.reason: Review of the Financial Statements and supporting schedules disclosed that RPT and SET receivables were already set up at the beginning of 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to submit original copies of duly accomplished distribution list in compliance with Section 4 of PD No. 1445 and to prove that there was indeed distribution made.
observations.managementAction: Distribution Lists were submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal Point to coordinate with the Local Finance Committee of the Municipality for the allocation of at least 5% of the total annual budget for the implementation of GAD-related activities as required under Section 36(a) of RA No. 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women.
observations.managementAction: AAR has aligned Gender and Development to various PPAs and allocated 5% to GAD PPAs.
observations.reason: The Municipality has allocated 5% to GAD PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation of Road Networks account following the straight line method in accordance to Paragraph 59 of IPSAS 17 and the accounting guidelines set under COA Cir. No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Municipal Engineer to identify the Road Networks and compute for the depreciation.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has not submitted the lapsing schedule for Road Networks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revert all outstanding balances of Accounts Payable for more than two years to Unappropriated Surplus pursuant to the above cited COA Circular.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant started to monitor the claims which are not supported with complete documents and will revert the Payable accounts at the end of the year.
observations.reason: There are still accounts payable that remained outstanding for more than two years. This is reiterated under Finding No. 4 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop using a privately-owned motor vehicle for official travel. Further, we recommended that management stops the practice of granting fuel to privately-owned vehicles and strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-004 to avoid wasteful spending of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Management had monitored the control on the issuance of Private motor gasoline, oil and vehicles are still utilized. We emphasized that issuing gas slips. using a private car for official travel is prohibited. Hence, fuel consumption for this is not allowed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit annually APP-CSE to PS-DBM within the prescribed deadline, thus helping PS-DBM in delivering more efficiently the requirements of the Municipality; and submit/attach a PS Certificate of Non-Availability of stocks if goods were purchase from a private dealer. Non submission of the document would compel the auditor to disallow the transaction pursuant to paragraph 4.9 of DBM Circular 2013-14. Also, strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 53.5 of IRR of RA No. 9184 and continue its practice of filing the agency procurement request to the PS-DBM as a modest contribution to achieve good governance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality did not purchase common use supplies from the PS-DBM due to the present pandemic situation. Rest However, when the situation return to normal Management will comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: The Audit Team is flexible and considerate in this pandemic situation. However, when the situation return to normal the recommendation will be enforced as a contributory factor to achieve good governance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management implement the GAD programs, projects and activities effectively and efficiently and fully utilized the GAD funds that was budgeted in accordance with its purpose that may address, strengthen and equalize the status and condition of relationships between men and women; identify and formulate within the time period and establish monitoring tools.
observations.managementAction: The Management had directed the MPDC to implement more GAD programs and projects not able to allocate at least 5% of the appropriation as GAD Fund.
observations.reason: Verification of records showed that the Management was not able to allocate at least 5% of the appropriation as GAD Fund. Reiterated under Finding No. 7 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to create an Appraisal Committee to appraise the two units donated ambulance and some equipment, furniture and fixtures in order for the Municipal Accountant to have a basis for the cost of the property to be recorded in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by management.
observations.reason: Management has not recognized in the books the two units donated ambulance and its accessories.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management utilize funds received to deliver the much-needed services or equipment to its constituents in accordance with issued guidelines and laws, for which the fund was granted and to avoid disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Motor Vehicle was purchased in good faith, However by the guidelines set by DILG was not followed. Management has continue to use the vehicle in accordance with ND No. 2019-001 (18) dated 5/18/2019 was issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management insure their properties to be indemnified in case of damages caused by fortuitous events.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer/GSO Designate had made the necessary procedures to apply for the insurance of the properties of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Review of records showed that properties not yet insured with the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE/Mun. Engineer explain in writing the circumstances surrounding the demolition of the building; We required the Management submit the Inventory and Inspection Report, photographs, as well as the Waste Material Report; and Management to strictly follow the guidelines on the divestment or for the disposal of property and other assets as provided under COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by management. We required the Municipal Engineer to submit the Waste Material Report for the Demolished Public Market but has not submitted as of this writing.
observations.reason: Demolished Public Market but has not submitted as of this writing. Reiterated in Finding No. 6 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal GSO to (a)
prepare an IIRPPE to be inspected and be assessed/appraised by the Agency
Appraisal and Inspection Team with the presence of the Auditor and submit the
same to the Municipal Accountant; and (b) cause for the disposal of unserviceable
properties but if found valuable the same be sold for public auction.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
inform the payees on their claims from the Municipality and revert to the
Government Equity those which are not filed with valid claims that have been
outstanding for two years or more.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
prepare and provide the Municipal Accountant a copy of the certified list of
taxpayers and the corresponding amount due and collectible for the year as the
basis of the latter in recording/book up the RPT and SET Receivables Accounts.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert
all oustanding balances of Accounts Payable that are not duly supported with
proper documentations and which have remained outstanding for more that two
years to the unapprpriated surplus pursuant to Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the GSO or Property Officer mantain a property card and the Municipal Accountant a subsidiary ledger card and to make a reconciliation periodically pursuant to the aforementioned Section C. 3, Chapter V of the Manual on Property Custodianship.
observations.managementAction: The GSO and the Municipal Accountant a Accountant maintained Property Card of each PPE and reconciled with periodically the Subsidiary Ledger of the books. Accounting Office.
observations.reason: Verified the maintained Property Card of each PPE and reconciled periodically the Subsidiary Ledger of the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant use the appropriate expense account in recording the transactions pertaining to the expenses incurred as assistance to NGAs assigned in the Municipality to present fair the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made adjustment and used the appropriate expense account in recording transactions pertaining to the expenses incurred as assistance to NGAs.
observations.reason: Verified records made and found substantial compliance of the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to strictly observe the completeness of the documents before payment to establish the validity of the transactions and to avoid the suspension or disallowances in audit. It is further recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Vice Mayor or other officials and employees reimburse to transportation allowance in actual fare or at the prevailing rates of the authorized mode of transportation from their permanent official station to the place of destination and vice versa in accordance with the Travel Law.
observations.managementAction: Management had instructed the Municipal Accountant to look into the records needed to support every transaction to prevent suspension or disallowances in audit.
observations.reason: Verification of documents substantiated the needed to support every transaction to prevent suspension or disallowances in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management through the BAC submit the print-out copies of the Notice of Awards and Notice to Proceed printed in the PhilGEPS Website to comply with the provisions of Section 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in order to establish transparency in the conduct of procurement activity.
observations.managementAction: The Management stressed that the print-out copies of the Notice of Awards and Notice to Proceed were in the BAC Files.
observations.reason: Verification of records substantiated the compliance with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the members of the BAC to adhere with the above-cited provisions pursuant to RA No. 9184 in order to establish transparency in the municipality’s procurement process. Further, we recommended that management submit the print-out copies of the Invitation to Bid and Notice of Award to establish completeness of the transaction.
observations.managementAction: Management directed the BAC to strongly adhere with the provisions to adhere with the records submitted the required documents.
observations.reason: Verification of records substantiated the compliance with the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere with the provisions of RA No. 9184 to attain the most advantageous price for all procurement projects of the Municipality. Further, we recommended that management strictly adhere with the provisions of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Management instructed the BAC and other concerend records individuals to strictly adhere with the provisions of RA No. 9184.
observations.reason: Verification of records substantiated the compliance with the provisions of RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere to the policies and guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC and Budget Officer stopped the charging of unrelated expenditures against the 20% DF.
observations.reason: Verification of records substantiated the compliance with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GSO or Property Officer mantain a property card and the Municipal Accountant a subsidiary ledger card and to make a reconciliation periodically pursuant to the aforementioned Section C. 3, Chapter V of the Manual on Property Custodianship.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation of Road Networks account following the straight line method in accordance to Paragraph 59 of IPSAS 17 and the accounting guidelines set under COA Cir. No. 2015-008.
We recommended that management revert all outstanding balances of Accounts Payable for more than two (2) years to Unappropriated Surplus pursuant to the above cited COA Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant use the appropriate expense account in recording transactions pertaining to the expenses incurred as assistance to NGAs assigned within the municipality to present fairly the financial statements.

We recommend further to make the necessary adjustment:
Subsidy to NGA				XXX
Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses		XXX

We recommended that Management stop using a privately-owned motor vehicle for official travel. Further, we recommended that management stop the practice of granting fuel to privately-owned vehicles and strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-004 to avoid wasteful spending of government funds.

We recommended that management strictly observe the completeness of the documents before payment to establish the validity of the transactions and to avoid the suspension or disallowances in audit.
It is further recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Vice Mayor or other officials and employees reimburse transportation allowance in actual fare or at the prevailing rates of the authorized mode of transportation from their permanent official station to the place of destination and vice versa in accordance with the Travel Law.

We recommended that management through the BAC submit the print-out copies of the Notice of Awards and Notice to Proceed printed in the PhilGEPS Website to comply with the provisions of Section 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in order to establish transparency in the conduct of procurement activity.

We recommended that Management require  the members of the BAC to adhere with the above-cited provision pursuant to RA 9184 in order to establuish transparency in the municipality’s procurement process.
Further, we recommended that management submit the print-out copies of the Invitation to Bid and Notice of Award to establish completeness of the transaction.
We recommended that management strictly adhere with the provisions of RA 9184 to attain the most advantageous price for all procurement projects of the municipality.

We recommended that Management:
a.  	Submit annually APP-CSE to PS-DBM within the prescribed deadline, thus helping PS-DBM in delivering more efficiently the requirements of the Municipality; and
b.  Submit/attach a PS Certificate of Non-Availability of stocks if goods were purchase from a private dealer.  Non submission of the document would compel the auditor to disallow the transaction pursuant to paragraph 4.9 of DBM Circular 2013-14.
Adhere with the provision of Section 53.5 of IRR of RA 9184 and continue its practice of filing the agency procurement request to the PS-DBM as a modest contribution to achieve good governance.
We recommended that management adhere to the policies and guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

We recommended the Management:
Implement the GAD programs, projects and activities effectively and efficiently and fully utilized the GAD funds that was budgeted in accordance with its purpose that may address, strengthen and equalize the status and condition of relationships between men and women.
Identify and formulate more GAD projects, programs and activities which shall be integrated in the Annual Investment Plan of the municipality within the context of its mandate.
Establish and strengthen in monitoring and evaluation the implementation of GAD projects, programs and activities.
We recommended that management continue with the prompt and regular remittance of withheld taxes within the period prescribed by the BIR Regulation.

For the remaining suspensions, we recommended that the concerned personnel and officials responsible and/or liable immediately settle the subject of their suspensions.  A follow up letter has been issued to liquidate the audit disallowance.

We recommended that the GSO-Designate coordinate with the Accountant to reconcile the result of the physical inventory and effect the necessary adjustments, where appropriate, in order to reflect the correct PPE balance as of December 31, 2018.
observations.managementAction: The GSO-Designate, Mun. Accountant had started to reconcile the difference of the balances of the Inventory Report with the PPE Account
observations.reason: Several PPE Account had not been reconciled from the Inventory Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the GSO-Designate to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) for inspection and appraisal. After which, the proper disposal will be determined and the corresponding value shall be dropped from the books in order to reflect the correct value of Property, Plant and Equipment in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The GSO-Designate made the inventory of the unserviceable properties and prepared the IIRUP
observations.reason: IIRUP was prepared but not yet dropped from the books of accouts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately liquidate advances granted to officials and employees in accordance to Section 5.7 to 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and COA Circular No. 2012-004(9.1).
observations.managementAction: The accountant had demanded the accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances
observations.reason: The amount of Php659,740.00 had been liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management grant cash advances for specific purpose to bonded employees only pursuant to COA Circular 2006-005 and as much as possible all payments must be made by check to avoid exposing government funds to possible loss.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped the granting of cash advances to unbonded officials since the 2nd quarter of the year.
observations.reason: All Accountable Officers are already bonded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management utilize funds received to deliver the much-needed services or equipment to its constituents in accordance with issued guidelines and laws, for which the fund was granted and to avoid disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: The management assured us to be more careful and strict in adhering to the laws and guidelines under the LGSF-AM.
observations.reason: Follow up letters had been issued to follow up with our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management insure their properties to be indemnified in case of damages caused by fortuitous events.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer/GSO Designate had made the necessary procedures to apply for the insurance of the properties
observations.reason: The GSO-Designate has not yet started applying for the insurance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee strictly adhere to the declared policy of the government for the promotion of good governance, the principles of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency and economy in its procurement process as provided under the procurement law.
observations.managementAction: The BAC had agreed to comply with our recommendation.
observations.reason: Several transactions of the municipality had not been posted in the PhilGeps website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant only process and certify the disbursement vouchers which are properly supported with complete documents as required under Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 in order to establish validity of claims and to avoid suspensions in post-audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had assured the Audit Team to strictly see to it that supporting documents are complete before processing such payments.
observations.reason: Several diabursement vouchers were not completely supported with the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop the practice of procuring goods on reimbursable basis, unless the circumstances warrant its application.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had disseminated the concerned employees the provisions of RA 9184 and the prohibition of procurement through reimbursement basis.
observations.reason: The agency had complied with our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere with the provisions of RA 9184 to attain the most advantageous price for all procurement projects of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Officers responsible were advised to make necessary measures to prevent the same findings in the next transactions.
observations.reason: Several deliveries of supplies and materials for the year 2019 were made before the conduct of procurement process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman and the BAC Secretariat should consolidate the procurement of similar or related items, with dates of requisition or request in close proximity to one another, into one purchase request and subject the same to competitive bidding in order to comply with the provision of IRR of RA 9184 and obtain the most advantageous price for the government.
observations.managementAction: The BAC reviewed the provisions of  RA 9184 to avoid purchasing in peacemeal basis.
observations.reason: The managemnet had complied with our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive implement strictly the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles prescribed in COA Cir. #77-61 dated September 26, 1977 for effective control of gasoline, oil and lubricants expenditures. Exercise control over the use of government vehicles through properly accomplished, duly approved and serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets. A summary of which shall be made at the end of the month in a Monthly Report of Official Travels for audit purposes.  Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption shall likewise be submitted to the auditor for verification purposes to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the period.
observations.managementAction: Management had conducted meetings with the drivers of the municipality and informed them on the provisions of COA Cir. No. 77-61 and instructed them to prepare the necessary documents.
observations.reason: Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption   were not submitted to the auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the refund of the amount of Financial Assistance granted to NGA personnel. Also, the management should strictly adhere with the provision of DBM Compensation Policy Guidelines for the payment of additional compensation in the form of honoraria chargeable against local government funds to NGA personnel assigned within the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management assured the Audit Team to stop the granting of financial assistance to employees of NGAs
observations.reason: Management complied with our recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:

The Local Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO), together with the concerned implementing offices, in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee, immediately look into the unimplemented projects under the 20% Development Fund and determine causes of their non-implementation. Formulate/develop specific strategies/solution to effectively implement development projects. Consider formulating a timetable within which to complete the unimplemented projects.

b.) The Offices concerned vigorously pursue the completion of the projects to be implemented the soonest time possible in order to achieve the objectives of the said projects in providing socio-economic benefits to the intended beneficiaries.

c.) The MPDO enhance monitoring of implementation of projects in coordination with the implementing offices and to submit regularly status reports of implementation to the Municipal Mayor for her information so that appropriate measures can be instituted to obtain optimal utilization of fund.
observations.managementAction: Management assured the Audit Team to comply with our recommendation.
observations.reason: Some priority projects are not yet completed on the expected date of completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere to the policies and guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
observations.managementAction: Management  adhered to the policies and guidelines in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: Disbursement for the payment of gasoline, oil and lubricants for dumptrucks and grass cutters had been stopped for the 2nd semester.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the implementation of the programs and projects so that timely benefits due to the constituents will likely be achieved or attained.
observations.managementAction: Management assured the Audit Team to comply with our recommendation.
observations.reason: Other priority projects funded by RA 7171 were not implemented due to limited of time and workforce.

97-002 and COA Circular No. 2012-004(9.1).

bonded employees only pursuant to COA Circular 2006-005 and as much as

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant only process and certify the

and to avoid suspensions in post-audit.
