We recommended management to create an Appraisal Committee to appraise the 2 units donated ambulance and some equipment, furniture and fixtures in order for the Municipal Accountant to have a basis for the cost of the property to be recorded in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by management.
observations.reason: The 2 units ambulance were not yet recorded in the books and inventory report of the management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
1. The LCE/Mun. Engineer explain in writing the circumstances surrounding the demolition of the building.
2. Management submit the Inventory and Inspection Report, photographs, as well as the Waste Material Report.
3. Management to strictly follow the guidelines on the divestment or disposal of property and other assets as provided under COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by management.
observations.reason: Demolished public market and stalls are still included in the books of accounts and inventory report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant facilitate the daily recording and timely submission of the monthly accounts in compliance with the above-cited provision in order to give the auditor reasonable time to determine the validity, propriety and completeness of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Employees concerned enhanced their daily recording in order to submit on time.
observations.reason: Not all accounts of the municipal government were submitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly enforce the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: There were still cash advances granted.
observations.reason: As of year end there were still unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management refrain from granting cash advances to non-bonded employees of the municipality. Likewise management cause the immediate bonding of Ms. Rowena S. Tabudlo, Social Welfare Officer III, because advances were regularly granted to her in the implementation of programs and activities.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped the granting of cash advances to unbonded officials since 2nd quarter of the year.
observations.reason: All Accountable Officers are already bonded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to stop the practice of granting cash advances to elective officials in conformity with Item 4.1.4 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped the practice of granting cash advances to elective officials.
observations.reason: Management had complied with our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prepare a realistic budget proposal to avoid excessive cash advances. Also require the AOs to liquidate their cash advance as soon as the purpose of which the cash advance was granted has been served.
observations.managementAction: Management had required the AO to refund the unutilized cash advances as soon as the purpose has been served.
observations.reason: Management minimized the granting of excessive cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions of Item 3 of National Budget Circular No. 563 dated April 22, 2016 and to refund the amount of P8,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Concerned officials are required to refund the excess of the traveling expenses.
observations.reason: Officials and employees committed to refund the excess.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that officials and employees who attended the seminar-workshop submit the documentary requirements needed in the payment of their traveling expenses.
observations.managementAction: Concerned officials required to submit their supporting documents.
observations.reason: Officials and employees committed to submit the documents lacking.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop the practice of splitting purchase requisitions/orders pursuant to COA Circular 76-41, to promote effective competition and transparency in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Agency agreed to implement our recommendation starting from the 2nd quarter of 2018.
observations.reason: Complied our recommendation starting from the 2nd quarter of 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that purchased supplies and materials should first be accepted by the general service officer/municipal treasurer before issuance to the requisitioning officer.
observations.managementAction: Agency required the GSO to receive the purchased supplies.
observations.reason: Materials purchased were directly delivered to end-user thus, not in agreement with Section 118, Volume I of NGAs Manual for LGUs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the requesting official and the inspectorate team accomplish the Pre-Repair Inspection Report properly and also prepare the Post-Repair Inspection Report. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant should see to it that all supporting documents required to support the claim were attached to the voucher.
observations.managementAction: The Pre-inspection report to completely accomplish.
observations.reason: Complied our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adhere strictly to the provisions of DSWD Administrative Order No. 5, Series of 2008, as amended by DSWD Administrative Order No. 1, Series of 2011 and Section 4(6) of P. D. 1445 for proper evaluation on the necessity and propriety of the financial assistance granted to individuals in crisis situation.
observations.managementAction: The municipality supported the transaction with documents.
observations.reason: Complied the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the GSO-Designate coordinate with the Accountant to reconcile the result of the physical inventory and to effect the necessary adjustments, where appropriate, in order to reflect the correct PPE balance as of December 31, 2017.

We recommended and management agreed to undertake the disposal procedures of unserviceable properties as mentioned in Section 79 of PD 1445 in order to reflect the correct value of Property, Plant and Equipment in the financial statements.

We recommended and management agreed to create an Appraisal Committee to appraise the 2 units donated ambulance and some equipment, furniture and fixtures in order for the Municipal Accountant to have a basis for the cost of the property to be recorded in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant facilitate the daily recording and timely submission of the monthly accounts in compliance with the above-cited provision in order to give the auditor reasonable time to determine the validity, propriety and completeness of the transactions.
We recommended that management strictly enforce the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that management refrain from granting cash advances to non-bonded employees of the municipality. Likewise management cause the immediate bonding of Ms. Rowena S. Tabudlo, Social Welfare Officer III, because advances were regularly granted to her in the implementation of programs and activities.
We recommended and management agreed to stop the practice of granting cash advances to elective officials in conformity with Item 4.1.4 of  COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended and management agreed to prepare a realistic budget proposal to avoid excessive cash advances. Also require the AOs to liquidate their cash advance as soon as the purpose of which the cash advance was granted has been served.
We  recommended that  management strictly comply with the provisions of  Item 3 of National Budget Circular No. 563 dated April 22, 2016 and to refund the amount of P8,000.00.
We recommended that officials and employees who attended the seminar- workshop submit the documentary requirements needed in the payment of their traveling expenses.

We recommended and management agreed to strictly observe the guidelines provided under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 and ensure that only development projects  be charged under the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of splitting purchase requisitions/orders pursuant to COA Circular 76-41, to promote effective competition and transparency in the procurement process.
We recommended and management agreed that purchased supplies and materials should first be accepted by the general service officer/municipal treasurer before issuance to the requisitioning officer.

We recommended and management agreed that the requesting official and the inspectorate team accomplish the Pre-Repair Inspection Report properly and also prepare the Post-Repair Inspection Report. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant should see to it that all supporting documents required to support the claim were attached to the voucher.

We recommended that management adhere strictly to the provisions of DSWD Administrative Order No. 5, Series of 2008, as amended by DSWD Administrative Order No. 1, Series of 2011 and Section 4(6) of P. D. 1445 for proper evaluation on the necessity and propriety of the financial assistance granted to individuals in crisis situation.

We have recommended, and management agreed, that the Municipality create an Appraisal Committee to appraise the 2 units donated ambulance and some equipment, furniture and fixtures in order for the Municipal Accountant to have a basis for the cost of the property to be recorded in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by management.
observations.reason: As of year end the value of the 2 units ambulance remained not recorded in the books of account and inventory report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended, and management agreed, that the agency reclassify the above mentioned equipment to inventory account and ascertain the existence of these properties in order to properly recognize in the books of accounts its actual value.
observations.managementAction: Purchases of small tangible items were already recorded to inventory account.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended, and management agreed,  that ICS for small tangible items be issued to end-users for easy monitoring, control and accountability.
observations.managementAction: Small tangible items were identified and informed the GSSO-Designate on the issuance of ICS to end –users. However purchases made during the year for small tangible items were already supported with ICS.
observations.reason: ICS were already attached to the disbursement vouchers on purchases made during the year for small tangible items.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere to the provisions of Section 114 of the NGAS in order to have a fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Purchased office supplies and medicines were recoded as inventories and charge to expense upon issuance.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended, and management agreed, that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries of the stale checks to correct the year-end balance of the cash in bank and accounts payable accounts for fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The municipality had identified the individual stale checks and the accounts where they were previously charged.
observations.reason: As of year end no adjustment had been made .

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management comply with the IRR of RA 9184 and as directed under DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011 in the procurement of common-use supplies of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The municipality did not purchase common use supplies from PS-DBM
observations.reason: Purchases of common-use supplies were made from suppliers in the locality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended, and management agreed, that Management stop the practice of splitting purchase requisitions/orders pursuant to COA Circular 76-41, to promote effective competition and transparency in the procurement process.
observations.reason: This finding is reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that each department head file in advance their requisitions for supplies needed for each quarter based on their approved Annual Procurement Plan. Likewise, the General Services Office consolidates the requisitions for each department in order to minimize procurement costs.
observations.managementAction: Management informed the department heads to file in advance their requisitions for office supplies and medicines
observations.reason: Purchase of medicines were already consolidated.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended, and management agreed, that the agency improve the controls and management of the agency’s procurement and consumption of fuel for its motor vehicles by adhering strictly to the regulations provided for under COA Circular No. 77-61.
observations.managementAction: Management had improved the controls and management of the agency’s procurement and consumption of fuel. Most of the DTT were already properly accomplished.
observations.reason: Monthly Reports of Official Travel and Fuel consumption were not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the agency compute the allowance on a per day basis as required under existing rules and regulations. We have likewise, recommended that an employee have a written authority from the LCE before he/she could avail of subsistence allowance for “on-call” days. We have further recommended that the agency implement a stronger control in its payroll system in order to avoid situations where employees are paid for days that they are not allowed to receive Subsistence Allowance.
observations.managementAction: Subsistence allowance will be granted on a per day basis.
observations.reason: The agency had started the payment of subsistence allowance based on the actual service of PHW.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the agency refrain and discontinue the grant of communication allowances to selected government officials and employees and adhere with the provisions of COA Circulars 2013-003 and 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The grant of communication allowance through payroll was discontinued.
observations.reason: Communication allowance was granted through the issuance of cell cards.

observations.recommendation:
We  have recommended that management:

1.Refrain from using savings or budget allocation for engineering supervision of implemented projects without prior approval from the local sanggunian; 

2.Strictly comply with the provisions of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3 dated November 29, 2007 on the payment of honoraria to government personnel involved in the procurement activities; and

3.Require the BAC and TWG members to refund the honoraria granted to them amounting to P136,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Payments were made from the proceeds of sale of bid documents.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended, and management agreed to instruct the SWO to require the personnel involved in the implementation of the program to submit their accomplishment reports before the payment of their honoraria.
observations.managementAction: Complete documentation and accomplishment reports were required from personnel involved in the ALS Program.
observations.reason: Accomplishment reports were already attached to the Disbursement vouchers of personnel involved in the program.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended, and management  agreed, that the Cash in Bank Balance maintained with the Philippine Veterans Bank be transferred to a GFI pursuant to DOF Department Circular NO. 001-2015.
observations.reason: Philippine Veterans Bank is already an authorized depository bank  per DOF Circular No. 001-2017 dated May 11, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipality refrain from entering into contracts with Full Hauz Grill and Catering and LGE Shell Gasoline Services for it is contrary to Section 47.1 and 47.2 of the RIRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The municipality had discontinued entering into contracts with Full Hauz Grill & Catering.
observations.reason: As with the purchase of gasoline from LGE Gasoline Service Station, purchases were necessarily made because it is the only gasoline station in the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the agency implement all projects under the 20% Development Plan during the year so that expected benefits due to beneficiaries/constituents will be achieved or delivered in order to uplift their living conditions.
observations.managementAction: Implemented some projects in 2017.
observations.reason: Some projects were already implemented by the municipality. However for the year 2017 most of the programmed projects were not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management require the contractors to post the Warranty Securities of the completed infrastructure projects to assure for the protection/guarantee against possible structural defects/failures that may occur during the applicable warranty period

We have recommended, and management agreed, that the Municipality create an Appraisal Committee to appraise the 2 units donated ambulance and some equipment, furniture and fixtures in order for the Municipal Accountant to have a basis for the cost of the property to be recorded in the books of accounts.

We have recommended, and management agreed, that the agency reclassify the above mentioned equipment to inventory account and ascertain the existence of these properties in order to properly recognize in the books of accounts its actual value.
We have recommended, and management agreed,  that ICS for small tangible items be issued to end-users for easy monitoring, control and accountability.
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere to the provisions of Section 114 of the NGAS in order to have a fair presentation of the financial statements.

We have recommended, and management agreed, that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries of the stale checks to correct the year-end balance of the cash in bank and accounts payable accounts for fair presentation of the financial statements.

We have recommended that management comply with the IRR of RA 9184 and as directed under DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011 in the procurement of common-use supplies of the municipality.

We have recommended, and management agreed, that Management stop the practice of splitting purchase requisitions/orders pursuant to COA Circular 76-41, to promote effective competition and transparency in the procurement process.

We have recommended that each department head file in advance their requisitions for supplies needed for each quarter based on their approved Annual Procurement Plan. Likewise, the General Services Office consolidates the requisitions for each department in order to minimize procurement costs.

We have recommended, and management agreed, that the agency improve the controls and management of the agency’s procurement and consumption of fuel for its motor vehicles by adhering strictly to the regulations provided for under COA Circular No. 77-61.

We have recommended that the agency compute the allowance on a per day basis as required under existing rules and regulations. We have likewise, recommended that an employee have a written authority from the LCE before he/she could avail of subsistence allowance for “on-call” days. We have further recommended that the agency implement a stronger control in its payroll system in order to avoid situations where employees are paid for days that they are not allowed to receive Subsistence Allowance.

We have recommended that the agency refrain and discontinue the grant of communication allowances to selected government officials and employees and adhere with the provisions of COA Circulars 2013-003 and 2012-001.

We  have recommended that management:
Refrain from using savings or budget allocation for engineering supervision of implemented projects without prior approval from the local sanggunian;
Strictly comply with the provisions of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3 dated November 29, 2007 on the payment of honoraria to government personnel involved in the procurement activities; and
Require the BAC and TWG members to refund the honoraria granted to them amounting to P136,000.00.

We have recommended, and management agreed to instruct the SWO to require the personnel involved in the implementation of the program to submit their accomplishment reports before the payment of their honoraria.
We have recommended, and management  agreed, that the Cash in Bank Balance maintained with the Philippine Veterans Bank be transferred to a GFI pursuant to DOF Department Circular NO. 001-2015.

We have recommended that the municipality refrain from entering into contracts with Full Hauz Grill and Catering and LGE Shell Gasoline Services for it is contrary to Section 47.1 and 47.2 of the RIRR of RA 9184.
We have recommended that the Municipality be guided by the PPSAS 17 in recognizing the value of these properties which will serve as their basis in recording the value of the donated properties.
observations.managementAction: No action taken, the   value of 2 units ambulance were not yet recorded in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Reiterated in in this report

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the General Services Officer-Designate  prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Properties. Determine also if the unserviceable and obsolete properties are valueless or unusable.  If so, these should be condemned and the corresponding cost or value should be dropped from the books in order to reflect the correct value of Property, Plant and Equipment in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The value of unserviceable properties were already dropped from the books  of accounts.
observations.reason: An inspection was conducted by COA TAS and a report was issued as basis in the dropping of the unserviceable properties

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the management to ensure strict compliance in the liquidation of cash advances, and adopt the following measures:

1.  Require all accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period as required under Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
	
2.  Cause or order the withholding of any money claims against the accountable officers concerned until they have fully settled their cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P215,780.00 was liquidated. Only 1 employee with a cash advance of P6,000.00 was not able to liquidate.
observations.reason: 97.29% of the prior year’s outstanding cash advances has been liquidated during the year

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Supply Officer-Designate and the BAC Secretariat to consolidate the procurement of similar or related items, with dates of requisition or request in close proximity to one another, into one purchase request and subject the same to competitive bidding.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented
observations.reason: Reiterated in finding No. 5, page 28 of the report

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the management to require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to submit copies of perfected contracts within the prescribed period to facilitate review and evaluation of the contracts.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: Some contracts and their supporting documents  were not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipality strictly adhere to the provisions of the DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103 and require the officials to refund the said amount.
observations.managementAction: The elective officials paid/refunded  the excess payment of their RATA
observations.reason: Payment has been made to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer on October 2016..

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the JEV for the reclassification of the recorded Buildings into Office Equipment and  Military and Security Equipment in the amount of P420,751.68 and P212,816.49, respectively, in order to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The recorded building was already reclassified into Office Equipment and  Military and Security Equipment.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant had prepared JEV No. 100-06-03-016G dated march 2016 to reclassify the PPE account.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management apply the fidelity bonds of the SWO III and the Municipal Mayor in compliance with Section 101 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The agency applied for the fidelity bond of the SWO III and the Mun. Mayor.
observations.reason: The Municipal Mayor and the SWO III were already bonded.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management insure their properties in order to be indemnified in case of damages caused by fortuitous events.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented
observations.reason: Some vehicles were already insured.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management require the contractors to post the Warranty Securities of the completed infrastructure projects to assure for the protection/guarantee against possible structural defects/failures that may occur during the applicable warranty period.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer exert more effort to determine the details of the Other Assets account by looking into the old files in their office and seek the assistance of their staff who had been in the agency for a longer period of time. We have also recommend that for succeeding transactions involving transfer of assets including property and equipment the Accounting Office should maintain subsidiary ledgers to support the details of the Other Assets account.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: The other assets account was recorded back to its specific asset account.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management comply with the  provisions of  Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 and Revenue Regulation No. 7-95.
observations.managementAction: Complied
observations.reason: Reimbursement of expenses were already subjected to tax.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the management to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF to the Trust Fund account in compliance with rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Complied
observations.reason: The amount was already transferred to the Trust Fund Account
